_comment: 2026-03-14 full study manifest. This file is used by the master report builder.
description: Comprehensive thesis-style study under the memory-aware protocol: stream subset and CAM memory subset are decoupled.

experiments.name: memory_sweep_external_ae16
experiments.title: Memory Capacity Sweep
experiments.question: When the test stream contains top50 units, how many units should CAM remember: top10, top20, top30, top40, or all top50?

experiments.name: update_compare_external_ae16
experiments.title: Dynamic Update Comparison
experiments.question: Under fixed stream=top50 and memory=top20, which dynamic CAM update strategy gives the best trade-off among accuracy, reject behavior, false accepts, and wrong updates?

experiments.name: bits_ablation_memoryaware
experiments.title: Bit-Budget Ablation
experiments.question: Under the same memory-aware protocol, how do 8 / 16 / 32 bits affect separability, reject behavior, and final CAM accuracy?

experiments.name: threshold_sweep_external_ae16
experiments.title: Reject Threshold Sweep
experiments.question: How does the Hamming threshold control the trade-off between false reject and false accept when memory is limited?

experiments.name: init_ablation_external_ae16
experiments.title: Template Initialization Ablation
experiments.question: How sensitive is the system to the initial template construction method under the memory-aware setting?

experiments.name: encoder_compare_memoryaware
experiments.title: Encoder Frontend Comparison
experiments.question: How much does the front-end encoder quality influence the downstream memory-aware CAM task?
